topic: Theme 3: Immigration and the Spanish-speaking Multicultural Society
subject: spanish

items.why: Students apply English pronunciation. Spanish H is ALWAYS silent. 'Hola' is 'OH-lah', not 'HO-lah'. (The breathy sound students associate with H comes from j or g+e/i in Spanish.)

items.q: Which preposition expresses 'for' as duration (e.g., 'for two hours')?
items.why: Students choose para because it's the default 'for'. POR is used for duration/extent of time. Para 'for two hours' would suggest a deadline ('to be ready BY two hours' time').

items.q: How is 'll' typically pronounced in Spanish?
items.why: Students pronounce 'll' as English double L. In most Spanish dialects, ll is a YOD sound (like English y) or a 'zh' sound (Argentina). Never pronounced as a doubled English L.

items.q: Which conjunction can take the subjunctive?
items.why: Correct answer: aunque. “porque” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: When did Spain become a net immigration country?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 1990s. “1980s” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many years of social integration are required for 'arraigo social' in Spain?
items.why: The real figure is 3 years. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
